title: Chronic Neonatal Respiratory Disorders
authors: Roberta L. Keller & Robin H. Steinhorn

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ BPD อย่างลึก?
BPD เกิดใน 40–70% ของ ELBW infants และเป็น strongest predictor ของ impaired neurodevelopment, respiratory morbidity, และ rehospitalization. (1) Definition เปลี่ยนมา 3 ครั้ง: Northway 1967 → NIH consensus 2000 → Jensen NRN Grade 1–3 (mode of support); (2) "New BPD" = alveolar simplification, not fibrosis — dysplastic arrested growth; (3) PVD/PH complication → mortality RR 4.7; (4) Caffeine (CAP trial): best pharmacological BPD prevention (NNT 10); (5) Post-discharge: 40–50% ใช้ bronchodilator ใน ปีแรก.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย BPD definition evolution: Northway 1967 → NIH 2000 (mild/moderate/severe) → Jensen NRN Grade 1–3
เข้าใจ epidemiology: incidence by GA (BPD/death 22–29 wk), risk factors (immaturity + adverse fetal environment)
อธิบาย 'new BPD' pathophysiology: alveolar simplification vs classic fibrosis/airway disease
รู้ role ของ PVD/PH ใน BPD: prevalence, mortality RR, echocardiographic markers
รู้ pharmacological prevention: caffeine (NNT 10), vitamin A (NNT 14), early hydrocortisone (NNT 13 + risks), iNO (racial differences), CPAP strategy
รู้ oxygen saturation targets: NeOProM meta-analysis — lower target (85–89%) → ↑ mortality + ↑ NEC แม้ ↓ BPD
อธิบาย post-discharge respiratory morbidity: wheeze, bronchodilator use, rehospitalization, obstructive lung disease
1. BPD Definition — Evolution
1.1 Northway 1967 — Classic BPD

Northway WH et al. N Engl J Med. 1967;276:357: First description: lung fibrosis + airway disease ใน preterm (mostly ~30 wk) after prolonged MV + hyperoxia. "Bronchopulmonary dysplasia" = dysplastic extrauterine lung development. Histology: fibrosis, inflammatory, airway lesions
1.2 NIH Consensus 2000 — Mild/Moderate/Severe
Jobe & Bancalari 2001: BPD defined at 36 weeks PMA in preterm < 32 wk. Tiers:
Mild BPD: room air at 36 wk
Moderate BPD: < 30% FiO2 at 36 wk
Severe BPD: ≥ 30% FiO2 and/or PPV at 36 wk
NICHD NRN validated: ↑ severity → ↑ rehospitalization, respiratory medication use, growth failure, NDI at 18–22 months (Ehrenkranz Pediatrics 2005)
1.3 Jensen NRN Grade 1–3 (Current Best Predictor)

Jensen Grade Classification (2019) — Validated Predictor of Outcome
Jensen EA et al. JAMA. 2019;321:1167. Defined solely by mode of respiratory support at 36 wk PMA (not FiO2): Grade 1: nasal cannula ≤ 2 LPM; Grade 2: NC > 2 LPM OR any noninvasive positive pressure; Grade 3: mechanical ventilation. Validated against death/severe respiratory morbidity at 18–26 months. Mode of support > FiO2 as predictor of outcome. Isayama (Canadian Neonatal Network): supplemental O2 OR PPV (including NC > 1.5 LPM) = strongest dichotomous predictor.

rows: NC > 2 LPM OR CPAP OR HFNC > 2 LPM
Died before 36 wk from lung disease

blocks.text: 2. Epidemiology — Who Gets BPD?
Primary risk: degree of immaturity (GA or BW). NICHD NRN 2008–2012: BPD among survivors at 36 wk PMA — 22 wk: 87.5%, 23 wk: 78.9%, 24 wk: 68.9%, 25 wk: 57.3%, 26 wk: 50.2%, 27 wk: 36.4%, 28 wk: 23.5%
Fetal/intrauterine environment: maternal vascular disorders (preeclampsia) + IUGR (BW z-score < -1) → ↑ BPD risk independent of GA. Maternal vascular underperfusion in placenta → ↑ BPD
FiO2 pattern early life: Laughon (ELGAN): consistently low FiO2 < 25% first 14 days → BPD incidence 17% vs high escalating FiO2 → 67% BPD
3. Pathophysiology — Classic vs New BPD
3.1 Classic BPD (Northway Era)
GA ~30 wk, after MV + hyperoxia: fibrosis, smooth muscle hypertrophy, airway metaplasia, pulmonary vascular changes, cor pulmonale; fixed + reactive airway obstruction
3.2 New BPD (< 28 wk Surfactant Era)

New BPD = arrested alveolar and microvascular development — born during late canalicular/early saccular stage; extrauterine arrest of normal lung growth. Histology: alveolar simplification (fewer, larger alveoli) + reduced capillary density; fibrosis LESS prominent
Yet: airway obstruction = most prominent clinical manifestation — wheeze, bronchodilator response; NOT explained by alveolar simplification alone → airway dysplasia co-exists
Additional contributors: PVD, infection susceptibility, environmental exposures, socioeconomic factors
4. PVD/PH as Co-Morbidity

PVD/PH in BPD — Mortality Risk
Arjaans meta-analysis (25 studies): PVD/PH prevalence in extremely preterm without BPD = 12%; with BPD = 20%; with severe BPD = 39%. Mortality RR = 4.7 (95% CI 2.7–8.3) with PVD/PH. Single center (Arjaans): suprasystemic RV pressure at 36 wk → 1-month survival 80%, 7-month 30% vs subsystemic → 94% and 76%. Vyas-Read (multicenter severe BPD): IVS flattening → mortality 19% vs 8% (normal echo).
Early markers: Mourani — IVS flattening at 7 days → ↑ BPD + ↑ PVD/PH at 36 wk. Mirza — delayed perinatal transition (RV pressure ≥ 50% systemic at 72–96 hr) = primary predictor of BPD or death
Vasoreactivity at cardiac catheterization: present in 35–77%; reactive (↓ PAP with vasodilator) → better outcome. O2 withdrawal → ↑ PA pressure = functional vasoconstriction component
SGA/IUGR ↑ PVD/PH risk independent of BPD — maternal vascular underperfusion in placenta → intrauterine lung + vascular injury
5. Oxygen Saturation Targets — NeOProM

NeOProM Meta-Analysis — Lower Target (85–89%) กับ Higher Target (91–95%)
NeOProM: 5 RCTs, N = 4965 extremely preterm (BOOST II, COT, SUPPORT, STOP-ROP, UKOS). Lower target (85–89%) vs higher (91–95%): ↑ Mortality at 36 wk PMA (risk difference 2.5%; 95% CI 0.5, 4.5%); ↑ Severe NEC (risk difference 2.3%); ↓ BPD (RR 0.81; 95% CI 0.74–0.90). Conclusion: targeting < 90% → ↑ mortality + NEC even if ↓ BPD. Current recommendation: SpO2 91–95% in preterm on supplemental O2 (Canadian practice); avoid lower range.
6. Pharmacological Prevention
6.1 Caffeine — Best Evidence (NNT 10)

CAP trial (Schmidt NEJM 2007): caffeine vs placebo: ↓ BPD OR 0.63; 95% CI 0.52–0.76; NNT 10; early initiation < 3 days more effective (Lodha JAMA Pediatr 2015). Mechanism: ↓ MV duration, ↓ O2 dependency, ↑ respiratory drive
6.2 Vitamin A

NICHD NRN RCT (Tyson NEJM 1999): Vit A 5000 IU IM 3×/wk × 4 wk in ELBW: ↓ BPD or death RR 0.89; NNT 14. Re-analysis: benefit concentrated in lowest risk quartile. IM injection 3×/wk = impractical; limited adoption
6.3 Early Hydrocortisone

Early Hydrocortisone — NNT 13 with Intestinal Perforation Risk
Meta-analysis (Doyle 2020 + IPD 2023): early hydrocortisone (first week): ↑ survival without BPD RR 1.19; NNT 13; ↓ BPD RR 0.84; NNT 14. But: ↑ intestinal perforation RR 1.76; NNH 28 — especially when combined with indomethacin for PDA. IPD analysis: perforation risk only held when co-administered with indomethacin. Also: ↑ late-onset sepsis OR 1.34 (NNH 15). Benefit on NDI: ↑ survival without moderate-severe NDI (RR 1.14; NNT 13). Conclusion: early hydrocortisone modest benefit but avoid concurrent indomethacin; individualize decision.
Dexamethasone: well-known ↑ cerebral palsy risk vs unexposed controls; risk modifiable by timing, dose, BPD risk level. Late dexamethasone (> 1 wk): ↓ extubation difficulty but NDI risk ongoing
Inhaled budesonide: ↓ BPD or death with marginal significance, but ↑ mortality at 18–22 months RR 1.37 (Bassler NEJM 2015) — unexplained; no recommended routine use
Budesonide + surfactant (Yeh NEJM 2016): instilled budesonide in calfactant ↓ BPD or death RR 0.58 in VLBW RDS; no NDI difference at 2–3 yr; limited to intubated RDS infants
6.4 iNO for BPD Prevention
Cochrane 2017 (various dosing trials): risk ratios 0.92–0.94, all CIs crossing null.

Individual patient data meta-analysis (racial differences): non-Hispanic whites: no benefit (RR 0.99); Black infants: significant benefit RR 0.77; NNT 7. Significant interaction p-value. Suggests racial/ethnic differences in response to iNO for BPD prevention.
6.5 CPAP Strategy vs MV

Cochrane 2016 (COIN + CURPAP + SUPPORT CPAP arms): CPAP from birth vs intubation + surfactant: ↓ BPD (RR 0.89; 95% CI 0.79–0.99) + ↓ BPD or death (RR 0.89). But longer total respiratory support duration and worse lung function at school age in CPAP-first strategy
7. Post-Discharge Respiratory Morbidity
BPD ≠ ends at 36 wk PMA. Post-discharge: wheeze/cough 29–58% (Marlow 2006 UKOS; Stevens 2014 SUPPORT; Keller 2017 TOLSURF)

Medication use first 1–2 yr after discharge: bronchodilator 40–51%; inhaled corticosteroids 22–56%; systemic steroids up to 16%. Netherlands population: respiratory medications 30% of < 32 wk at 6–12 months vs 16% term controls

Long-term: obstructive lung disease = most prominent long-term morbidity — FEV1/FVC ↓, bronchodilator response, asthma diagnosis in former extreme preterm. Dysplastic airway development independent from alveolar simplification
Ryan PROP study (2019): medication exposure in first year decreases with ↑ GA. Severe BPD (BPD Collaborative cohort): home respiratory support 44%, rehospitalization 55% in first 2 yr
8. NICU Practical — Managing BPD Risk
FiO2 tracking: document cumulative O2 exposure first 14 days — ↑ O2 exposure pattern = ↑ BPD risk (Laughon ELGAN). Target SpO2 91–95%
Caffeine: start ≤ 3 days of age ใน all < 32 wk. Loading 20 mg/kg, maintenance 5–10 mg/kg q24h. Can extend beyond 34–36 wk ถ้า still on respiratory support
MV strategy: minimize invasive ventilation — CPAP first, surfactant via LISA, extubate early. Target VT 4–6 mL/kg, permissive hypercapnia (PaCO2 45–55)
Nutrition: ↑ protein 3.5–4 g/kg/day + adequate calories → support lung growth. Vitamin A IM ถ้า available/feasible in ELBW
PVD/PH monitoring: echo at day 7 (IVS flattening = early PH marker); repeat at 36 wk PMA. ถ้า PH: avoid hypoxia (maintain SpO2 > 93–95%), sildenafil, evaluate for ECMO ถ้า severe
Discharge planning: families ของ infant severe BPD: expect home O2 (40%), bronchodilators (50%), re-hospitalization (55%). RSV prophylaxis with palivizumab ใน eligible infants
9. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — BPD Grade = Mode of Support, Not FiO2
Jensen NRN Grade ขึ้นกับ mode of support ไม่ใช่ FiO2 ที่ 36 wk PMA: Grade 1 = NC ≤ 2 LPM; Grade 2 = NC > 2 LPM หรือ CPAP; Grade 3 = MV. Mode of support = stronger predictor ของ long-term outcome. เวลา counsel families: Grade 3 = highest risk for neurodevelopmental impairment + respiratory rehospitalization.

PEARL 2 — NeOProM: SpO2 < 90% → ↑ Mortality ↑ NEC แม้ ↓ BPD
Lower target 85–89% ↓ BPD แต่ ↑ mortality (risk difference 2.5%) + ↑ NEC (2.3%). ดังนั้น target SpO2 91–95% ใน preterm on supplemental O2 — ไม่ต้องการ BPD definition improvement ที่แลกมาด้วยชีวิต.

PEARL 3 — Early Hydrocortisone: ห้ามให้พร้อม Indomethacin
Early hydrocortisone NNT 13 สำหรับ BPD prevention แต่ NNH 28 สำหรับ intestinal perforation เฉพาะเมื่อให้ร่วมกับ indomethacin. IPD analysis ยืนยัน: ถ้าไม่มี indomethacin → perforation risk ไม่ significant. ดังนั้น ถ้าจะใช้ early hydrocortisone → ไม่ให้ indomethacin ในเวลาเดียวกัน

PEARL 4 — PVD/PH ใน BPD: ตรวจ Echo ที่ Day 7 + 36 wk
IVS flattening ที่ day 7 (Mourani) = early marker ↑ BPD + PVD/PH ที่ 36 wk. Severe BPD + suprasystemic RV pressure → mortality 39% (single center). Echo ที่ 36 wk PMA routine ใน all BPD Grade 2–3. ถ้า PVD/PH: sildenafil 0.5–2 mg/kg/dose q6–8h; avoid acute hypoxia; ECMO criteria ถ้า refractory.

PEARL 5 — iNO for BPD: Benefit in Black Infants, Not in White
Individual patient data meta-analysis: iNO for BPD prevention ได้ผลใน Black infants (RR 0.77, NNT 7) แต่ไม่ได้ผลใน non-Hispanic whites (RR 0.99). ดังนั้น iNO สำหรับ BPD prevention ไม่ใช่ indication ทั่วไป — ควรพิจารณาเฉพาะ Black infants ที่ high risk. Routine use ไม่แนะนำ.
10. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Northway WH et al. N Engl J Med. 1967;276:357: First BPD description — lung fibrosis + airway disease after MV + hyperoxia in preterm.
Jobe AH, Bancalari EH. Am J Respir Crit Care Med. 2001;163:1723 (NIH consensus): BPD defined at 36 wk PMA: mild/moderate/severe; 'new BPD' concept (alveolar simplification).
Jensen EA et al. JAMA. 2019;321:1167: NICHD NRN Grade 1–3 definition by mode of support at 36 wk — mode > FiO2 as outcome predictor.
Schmidt B et al. N Engl J Med. 2007;357:1893 (CAP): Caffeine ↓ BPD OR 0.63 (NNT 10); ↓ O2 dependency, ↓ MV duration.
NeOProM Collaborative. Pediatrics. 2018;142:e20180903: Lower SpO2 target (85–89%) vs higher (91–95%): ↓ BPD (RR 0.81) but ↑ mortality (risk diff 2.5%) + ↑ NEC. Current standard: 91–95%.
Doyle LW et al. Cochrane 2020;CD001145 (early hydrocortisone): ↑ survival without BPD (NNT 13); ↑ intestinal perforation NNH 28 with concurrent indomethacin.
Tyson JE et al. N Engl J Med. 1999;340:1962 (Vitamin A): Vit A 5000 IU IM 3×/wk in ELBW: ↓ BPD or death RR 0.89; NNT 14.
Bassler D et al. N Engl J Med. 2015;373:1497 (inhaled budesonide): ↓ BPD or death marginal; ↑ mortality at 18–22 months RR 1.37 — unexplained; not recommended routinely.
Manja V et al. Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal. 2015 (iNO racial meta-analysis): iNO benefit for BPD prevention: Black infants RR 0.77 (NNT 7); no benefit non-Hispanic whites.
Arjaans S et al. Neonatology. 2019;115:185 (PVD/PH meta-analysis): PVD/PH prevalence in BPD 20–39%; mortality RR 4.7 (2.7–8.3); SGA ↑ risk independent of BPD.
Mourani PM et al. J Pediatr. 2015;166:600: IVS flattening at 7 days → ↑ BPD + PVD/PH at 36 wk; early echocardiographic screening warranted.
Laughon M et al. Pediatrics. 2009;123:1292 (FiO2 pattern): Consistently low FiO2 < 25% at day 1–14 → BPD incidence 17% vs high/escalating FiO2 → 67%.
11. Self-Check Questions
Q: BPD Grade 1/2/3 ตาม Jensen definition — แบ่งตาม criteria อะไร?

ตอบ: Jensen NRN (JAMA 2019): แบ่งตาม mode of respiratory support ที่ 36 wk PMA ไม่ใช่ FiO2: Grade 1 = nasal cannula ≤ 2 LPM; Grade 2 = NC > 2 LPM หรือ CPAP หรือ HFNC > 2 LPM; Grade 3 = mechanical ventilation; Grade IIIA = died before 36 wk from lung disease. Mode of support เป็น stronger predictor ของ death/severe respiratory morbidity ที่ 18–26 months มากกว่า FiO2.
Q: อธิบาย pathophysiology ของ 'new BPD' ว่าต่างจาก classic BPD อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Classic BPD (Northway): preterm ~30 wk หลัง MV + O2 → fibrosis, smooth muscle hypertrophy, airway metaplasia. New BPD (< 28 wk, surfactant era): born ใน late canalicular/early saccular stage → extrauterine arrested alveolar and microvascular development = alveolar simplification (fewer, larger alveoli + ↓ capillary density). Fibrosis LESS prominent. Yet airway obstruction = dominant clinical manifestation — wheeze + bronchodilator response (airway dysplasia co-traveler, not explained by alveolar simplification alone).
Q: NeOProM: oxygen target ในใน preterm ควรเป็นเท่าไหร่? basis?

ตอบ: Target SpO2 91–95%. NeOProM 5-trial IPD meta-analysis (n=4965): lower target 85–89% vs higher 91–95%: ↓ BPD (RR 0.81) BUT ↑ mortality (risk diff 2.5%) + ↑ severe NEC (risk diff 2.3%). Trade-off: modest ↓ BPD ไม่คุ้มกับ ↑ death + NEC. Lower range = avoid. SGA infants had even greater BPD benefit from lower targets (RR 0.54) but mortality risk remains.
Q: Early hydrocortisone สำหรับ BPD prevention — evidence และ risks?

ตอบ: NNT 13 สำหรับ survival without BPD. Risk: intestinal perforation NNH 28 — BUT only when co-administered with indomethacin (IPD analysis). NDI benefit: ↑ survival without moderate-severe NDI (RR 1.14, NNT 13). Also: ↑ late-onset sepsis (OR 1.34, NNH 15). Decision: individualize — use in high-risk BPD patients; avoid concurrent indomethacin. Late hydrocortisone (> 1 wk): insufficient data.
Q: PVD/PH ใน BPD: ทำไมสำคัญ? monitoring strategy?

ตอบ: PVD/PH = mortality RR 4.7. Prevalence: ~20% ในทุก BPD; ~39% ใน severe BPD. Suprasystemic RV pressure at 36 wk → 7-month survival 30%. Monitoring: echo day 7 (IVS flattening = early marker); echo at 36 wk PMA for all Grade 2–3 BPD. Treatment: optimize O2 (SpO2 > 93–95%); avoid acute desaturation; sildenafil (PDE5i) 0.5–2 mg/kg q6–8h PO; bosentan (ET-A/B antagonist); ECMO criteria ถ้า refractory.
12. Day 44 Preview

Avery Chapter 44 — Anatomic Disorders of the Chest and Airways
พรุ่งนี้เรียน Anatomic Disorders of the Chest and Airways — surgical neonatology. Chapter ครอบคลุม: upper airway (choanal atresia, CHARGE, Pierre Robin, laryngomalacia, VCP, subglottic stenosis), tracheal disorders (tracheomalacia, tracheal stenosis, vascular rings), chest anomalies (CPAM, pulmonary sequestration, CDH), mediastinal masses, EXIT procedure. เชื่อมกับ Ch43: BPD preterm → tracheomalacia (iatrogenic from MV); CDH → pulmonary hypoplasia + new BPD-like chronic lung disease post-repair.
